description_long: This is a two-tined pitchfork with a long, straight handle and sharply pointed prongs. The tines are evenly spaced and extend upward in a V-shape, making it suitable for lifting or turning materials such as hay, leaves, or debris. The handle appears to be solid and robust, likely made of metal, and features two subtle bands near the base for added grip or reinforcement. The entire object is a dark, matte color, giving it a utilitarian and durable appearance. The tines are slightly tapered, enhancing their ability to penetrate and lift materials. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or additional attachments on the handle or tines.
description: A dark, two-tined pitchfork with a long, straight metal handle and sharply pointed prongs. The design is simple, sturdy, and utilitarian.
description_view_0: Front view showing both tines in a V-shape and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the tines and handle, with both tines aligned.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, displaying the two tines and straight handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and alignment of the tines.
